channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Fala de Flávio Bolsonaro nos EUA sobre terras raras revolta a esquerda
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) sparked controversy after stating at a U.S. conservative event (CPAC) that Brazil could reduce U.S. dependence on China for rare earths and critical minerals, while urging international monitoring of Brazilian elections. His remarks were condemned by Lula administration allies as a betrayal of national sovereignty, accusing him of offering Brazil's natural wealth to foreign powers in exchange for political support.

item.title: O que acontece agora após o fim da CPMI do INSS sem relatório aprovado
item.description: The INSS CPI concluded without approving its final report, which recommended indicting 216 people, including President Lula’s son. Despite the rejection, the rapporteur said he will forward the document to the PF, TCU, MP, and Federal Revenue. In parallel, the government base produced an alternative report calling for the indictment of 130 people, including Jair and Flávio Bolsonaro, which will also be sent to authorities.

item.title: Lula é tão desaprovado quanto Bolsonaro a 6 meses da eleição
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva shows disapproval ratings similar to those of Jair Bolsonaro at the same point in their mandates (3 years and 3 months), with 51% negative and 26% positive evaluations, according to PoderData. Both are the only incumbents since redemocratization to run for re-election amid high rejection, with Bolsonaro being the sole one to lose. Lula now holds a record 61% disapproval in binary response polling, signaling significant public wear despite not facing a pandemic like his predecessor.

item.title: Crise política no Rio: entenda por que o estado terá eleição para mandato-tampão e o impasse no STF
item.description: Rio de Janeiro will hold a supplemental election for governor after the resignations of Governor Cláudio Castro and Vice Governor Thiago Pampolha, leaving the state executive vacant until the end of 2026. The state legislature (Alerj) approved an indirect vote by state deputies, but the PSD challenged the rule in the Supreme Federal Court (STF), leading Minister Cristiano Zanin to suspend the indirect election and demand an in-person review. The STF plenary is expected to rule during the second week of April, with a date yet to be set by President Luiz Edson Fachin.

item.title: Eduardo diz gravar vídeo para o pai em congresso da direita, mas Moraes barrou celular
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro recorded a video with his phone during a right-wing conference in the United States on Saturday, the 28th, intending to show it to his father, Jair Bolsonaro, before his brother Flávio Bolsonaro's speech. According to the account, Justice Alexandre de Moraes of the STF had barred cellphone use at the event, although the video was recorded beforehand.

item.title: ‘Dancinhas’ de Flávio Bolsonaro não devem render votos
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's dance video garnered 54 million views, but experts say it won’t translate into votes, as it mainly engages captive audiences. The strategy aims to humanize his image, inspired by Javier Milei, but is criticized for lacking naturalness. Despite high reach, analysts warn the informal tone may not resonate with more discerning voters.

item.title: Questionado sobre Flávio, Zema descarta vice e ataca políticos
item.description: Former Minas Gerais governor Romeu Zema (Novo) ruled out withdrawing from the presidential race to become vice-president on a ticket with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), stating he intends to pursue his independent candidacy. He also indirectly criticized politicians aligned with former President Jair Bolsonaro, reinforcing his distancing from bolsonarism.

item.title: Nenhuma 3ª via deu certo em 9 eleições para presidente
item.description: In the 9 presidential elections since 1989, no 'third way' candidate has succeeded, reinforcing that left-right polarization is structural in Brazilian politics. The 1989 election was the only one with a competitive third candidate, but even then, tucano Mario Covas finished fourth with no real chance of winning. Current polls suggest the 2026 race is locked between Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), making a viable centrist alternative unlikely.

item.title: PT já vê MDB e PSD fora da chapa de Lula
item.description: PT national president Edinho Silva stated that the MDB and PSD parties are unlikely to join Lula's national re-election alliance in 2026, noting any agreements with these parties should occur only at the state level. He also mentioned efforts to attract the MDB through direct talks with Lula and offers of cabinet positions.

item.title: A condição da PGR para que Daniel Vorcaro obtenha benefícios judiciais ao delatar a República
item.description: The Attorney General's Office (PGR), led by Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet, has set a condition for Daniel Vorcaro to receive judicial benefits in a potential plea bargain: he must provide new facts about his illegal dealings with politicians, public servants, Republic authorities, and financial market businessmen. Information already obtained by the Federal Police through searches and forensics will not count toward sentence reduction. Vorcaro is detained in the PF Superintendence in Brasília, in the same room previously used by Jair Bolsonaro, and has begun working with his lawyers on the collaboration.

item.title: Zema sustenta candidatura: ‘Até Bolsonaro avalia que quanto mais candidatos à direita melhor’
item.description: Romeu Zema says he will maintain his presidential candidacy for 2026 despite resigning as governor of Minas Gerais. He claims Jair Bolsonaro personally advocated that having more right-wing candidates is beneficial for a second-round runoff against Lula. Zema denies receiving a formal invitation to be Flávio Bolsonaro’s running mate and argues the right will have multiple options in the first round but unite in the second.

item.title: Brasil 2026: o país do bingo permanente (por Gaudêncio Torquato)
item.description: Brazil in 2026 exists in a state of permanent uncertainty, defined by polarization, corruption scandals, and economic strain, where politics has devolved into symbolic battles and voters are becoming more pragmatic, prioritizing health, security, and the economy. The perception that the system functions as designed — not broken — deepens institutional distrust.

item.title: Clarita Maia na Crusoé: Não são só candidatas
item.description: Gender-based political violence in Brazil primarily protects female candidates and officeholders, but this narrow focus is inadequate since politics extends beyond elections into networks and informal spheres of power. The author argues for broadening protections to include all women engaged in political life, regardless of elected office.

item.title: Presidente do PT admite MDB fora da chapa de Lula e defende foco em alianças nos estados
item.description: PT national president Edinho Silva admits that MDB and PSD will not be part of Lula’s national ticket for the 2026 re-election bid, advocating that alliances with these parties should be built at the state level. He stresses the need to build a strong democratic field to defeat fascism and focuses on consolidating ties with historical allies, despite internal resistance in states like Rio Grande do Sul.

item.title: Sem reunião entre Lula e Trump, acordo contra crime pode travar
item.description: The Brazilian government believes a meeting between Lula and Trump must occur by July to prevent the U.S. State Department from unilaterally designating the PCC and Comando Vermelho as terrorist organizations, a move Brazil opposes. Without direct presidential engagement, Brazilian officials argue that U.S. technical interlocutors lack decision-making authority, and factions tied to Bolsonaro may be influencing the U.S. push. The window is narrowing due to Brazil’s electoral calendar and Trump’s focus on the Iran conflict.

item.title: Brasil é ‘fracasso’ e precisa de nova Constituição, diz Kataguiri
item.description: Federal Deputy Kim Kataguiri (SP) advocated for discussing a new Federal Constitution, stating that the 1988 Constitution no longer reflects the spirit of the times and that Brazil has been a 'failure' for at least 40 years. He criticized the Bolsonaro government for wasting the hope of a true political revolution and mentioned his possible candidacy for a third term in Congress or for governor of São Paulo, while also supporting Renan Santos' presidential bid.

item.title: “Lula e seu partido são abertamente antiamericanos”, diz Flávio na CPAC
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, a presidential pre-candidate from the PL party, claimed at CPAC that Lula and the PT are openly anti-American, criticizing the president for publicly discussing undermining the dollar as a global currency. He also incorrectly compared Lula to Venezuelan dictator Nicolás Maduro, falsely stating that Maduro is imprisoned in New York.

item.title: Flávio diz que PL da Misoginia foi uma armadilha do PT contra ele
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) defended his favorable vote on Bill 896/2023, dubbed the 'Misogyny Bill', as a PT trap designed to damage his image among female voters in an election year. He stated he voted in favor because the bill can still be amended in the Chamber of Deputies and that he had no opportunity to propose changes in the Senate. Despite his justification, Bolsonarist allies criticized the vote, viewing it as a concession to censorship and a threat to freedom of expression, particularly on social media.

item.title: O impacto das eleições de meio de mandato nos EUA para os investidores
item.description: U.S. midterm elections have negatively impacted American stock markets and Bitcoin since 2018, according to Binance Research, though prices have typically rebounded with strong gains in the three years following each election. As the 2026 midterms approach in November, Brazilian investors seeking diversification are advised to remain calm and composed. The article emphasizes that electoral volatility is a recurring pattern, not a sign of enduring crisis.

item.title: Gleisi chama Flávio e Eduardo de “vendilhões da pátria” após discurso no CPAC
item.description: Chief Minister of Institutional Relations Gleisi Hoffmann (PT) harshly criticized Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) and his brother Eduardo Bolsonaro for attending CPAC, the major U.S. conservative event, calling them 'traitors of the homeland' in a post on X. The remark followed Flávio's speech at the event, highlighting the growing tension between the Lula administration and the Bolsonaro opposition.

item.title: Na batalha digital entre Lula e Flávio, entra em cena a disputa da vitalidade física
item.description: Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro are using videos of physical activity to project vitality amid the 2026 electoral dispute. Lula’s workout video at age 80 aims to counter health rumors, while Flávio’s dance in Rio Grande do Norte seeks to soften his image and attract youth support. The digital clash highlights age as an indirect campaign theme, with Lula seeking to demonstrate stamina for a potential fourth term at age 85.

item.title: Jogo pesado: os ‘pontos fracos’ que Haddad e Tarcísio vão explorar na campanha
item.description: The campaign between Fernando Haddad (PT) and Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) in São Paulo is already focused on attacking the opponent's weaknesses, with the PT criticizing Sabesp's privatization, the 'free flow' toll model, and investigations against Vice Governor Felicio Ramuth, plus the Banco Master scandal. Conversely, Tarcísio's campaign aims to link Haddad to tax increases under Lula's government, highlight the PT's corruption history (Mensalão, Petrolão), and emphasize Haddad's electoral losing streak, including his 2022 defeat to Tarcísio.

item.title: Distribuidoras de energia podem ter crédito de R$ 7 bi
item.description: The government is evaluating a credit line of up to R$ 7 billion via BNDES for electricity distributors in the South, Southeast, and Center-West regions to ease tariff hikes in an election year, while R$ 7.87 billion from the repayment of Public Use Asset (UBP) fees will be directed to consumers in the North and Northeast. The initiative aims to mitigate the impact of tariff increases, which have risen 45% in real terms over 15 years, outpacing inflation.

item.title: Gleisi diz que Flávio Bolsonaro faz “juras de subserviência” a Trump
item.description: Institutional Relations Minister Gleisi Hoffmann accused Flávio Bolsonaro of making 'oaths of subservience' to Donald Trump during a CPAC speech in the U.S., claiming he and his brother Eduardo are conspiring to deliver Brazil to foreign interests. Flávio criticized Lula, alleging the president lobbied U.S. officials to prevent the PCC and Comando Vermelho from being designated as terrorist organizations, and advocated for a foreign policy aligned with U.S. interests.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro copia Trump e quer mídia como paga-lanche de campanha
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign is attempting to use the media as a paid promotional tool, generating factoid-like content within 24 hours to boost visibility. This strategy mirrors tactics used by Donald Trump and requires media outlets to stay vigilant to avoid becoming mere campaign payroll vehicles.

item.title: Plano econômico de Zema inclui privatizações e jornada flexível
item.description: Presidential pre-candidate Romeu Zema (Novo) unveiled his economic plan on April 16, proposing the full privatization of all state-owned enterprises — including Petrobras, Banco do Brasil, and Caixa Econômica Federal — and an hourly wage-based work schedule with no maximum limit. The proposal, reported by Folha de S.Paulo, is being coordinated by his economic team.

item.title: Ibaneis Rocha assina renúncia no DF e deve se candidatar ao Senado
item.description: Ibaneis Rocha (MDB), governor of the Federal District, resigned on Saturday to run for the Senate in the 2026 elections, complying with the six-month disqualification requirement before the vote. His deputy, Celina Leão (PP), will assume the governorship on an interim basis. The resignation comes amid a financial crisis involving the Bank of Brasília (BRB), which sought a R$4 billion loan from the FGC using public assets as collateral, some of which are under judicial challenge, including the Serrinha do Paranoá area.

item.title: Em 1 ano, Master vai de compra pelo BRB a acordo de Vorcaro com PF
item.description: One year after the announcement of the BRB's purchase of Banco Master, founder Daniel Vorcaro is imprisoned and has signed a confidentiality agreement with the Federal Police and the PGR in March 2026, enabling a potential plea bargain that could reach the Three Powers. Banco Master, together with Will Bank and Banco Pleno, caused a record R$ 52 billion shortfall in the FGC despite holding less than 1% of the national financial system's assets. Investigations allege that BRB purchased toxic assets from Master, triggering a Central Bank intervention in November 2025 and a cascade of political repercussions involving the STF, Congress, and CPI.

item.title: Lula é rejeitado por geração Z, e crescimento de Renan Santos, do MBL, preocupa governo
item.description: Recent polls show that nearly 73% of young voters aged 16 to 24 disapprove of President Lula, a 14-point increase from the previous month. Meanwhile, Renan Santos, the candidate from the small Missão party and ally of the MBL, has surged among Generation Z, rising from 2.9% to 24.7% in voting intentions within this demographic, appealing to youth with his direct and anti-bureaucratic rhetoric. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro also leads among young voters with 37% preference, surpassing Lula’s 28.6%, signaling a growing right-wing shift among Brazil’s youth electorate.

item.title: Renan Santos vira ‘Milei brasileiro’ para disputar a direita com Flávio, a quem chama de ‘Judas’
item.description: Renan Santos, a presidential pré-candidate and founder of the MBL, calls himself the 'Brazilian Javier Milei' due to his disruptive style, criticism of Bolsa Família, and strong social media presence. Despite lacking electoral experience, he reached 4.6% in voting intentions in the Atlas/Bloomberg poll, with strong support among youth (24.7%) and men (7.7%), and positions himself as an opponent to Flávio Bolsonaro, whom he calls 'Judas'.

item.title: Fundo Partidário: voto para deputado define recursos que cada partido recebe
item.description: Brazil's Party Fund distributes public resources based on parties' electoral performance in the Chamber of Deputies election: 95% are allocated proportionally to votes and elected deputies, while 5% are divided equally among all registered parties. Access to the fund is conditional on the performance clause, requiring parties to obtain at least 3% of valid national votes to qualify, directly linking citizen votes to parties' financial capacity.

item.title: Brasil tem aumento de 22,7 milhões no grupo dos inadimplentes
item.description: Brazil saw an increase of 22.7 million people in default between 2016 and 2026, reaching 81.7 million, according to a Serasa report. Default is concentrated in debts with banks and financial institutions (47.1%) and basic accounts (21.4%), with 42% of defaulters having been blacklisted for over 10 years. The Central Bank highlights credit cards as a key factor, with revolving interest rates reaching up to 1,216.55% per year.

item.title: Série D: campeões retornam em busca de acesso e nova conquista
item.description: The 2026 Brasileirão Série D kicks off on April 4 with 96 clubs, including eight former champions aiming for promotion to Série C and a new title. The tournament features a regionalized first phase with 16 groups of six teams, followed by a knockout stage, with the final on September 6 and 13. Six teams will earn promotion to Série C 2027.

item.title: Dívida das famílias indica limites do modelo petista de governar
item.description: Lula's third term included the Desenrola Brasil program, which offers special conditions for renegotiating debts of individuals with negative credit, and established a cap limiting accumulated credit card interest to 100% of the original loan amount, adopting an English model. These measures aim to alleviate household debt but also reflect the limits of the Petista model under persistent economic pressure.

item.title: Flávio faz dancinhas para mirar TikTok e jovens da periferia, mas aliados rejeitam iniciativa
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for President, launched the jingle 'Funk do 01' and began dancing at pre-campaign events to attract peripheral youth and gain traction on TikTok. Inspired by hits like 'A Dança do Créu', the strategy aims to use funk culture to connect with young voters, but allies have rejected the initiative, deeming it inappropriate or ineffective for a presidential candidate.

item.title: Moderação alardeada por Flávio esbarra em projeto bolsonarista e no seu passado político
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro is attempting to position himself as a moderate politician to broaden his electoral appeal in a potential 2026 presidential bid, distancing himself from the more inflammatory style of his father, Jair Bolsonaro. However, this moderate image is challenged by his political record aligned with Bolsonarism and skepticism from allies regarding his ideological consistency.

item.title: Flávio afirma que Lula “faz lobby” para proteger cartéis brasileiros
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro accused President Lula of lobbying U.S. officials to prevent Brazil’s two largest drug cartels from being designated as terrorist organizations, claiming this protects groups that oppress the Brazilian people. He made the claim during a speech at CPAC in Texas, citing a New York Times article that actually showed he and his brother Eduardo had pressured the Trump administration to designate the PCC and Comando Vermelho as terrorists. Flávio also labeled the Lula government as 'openly anti-American,' linking it to economic, security, and corruption crises.

item.title: Flávio exibe foto de Maduro abraçado a Lula em evento nos EUA
item.description: Presidential pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro spoke at CPAC in the U.S., displaying a photo of Lula embracing Maduro and accusing the Brazilian president of lobbying to protect criminal organizations, while declaring Brazil faces a devastating economic crisis and expansion of narcoterrorist cartels. He also stated Brazil could ally with the U.S. to supply rare minerals to reduce dependence on China, and was met with boos when showing the Lula-Maduro image, while the crowd chanted his name when he claimed he would win the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: PT enviará relatório paralelo ignorado por CPI para PF e STF
item.description: The PT announced it will submit a parallel report to the Federal Police and the Supreme Court after the INSS CPI rejected the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva and ignored the party's alternative report. The PT's report, over 1,800 pages led by Rogério Correia, expands investigations to 201 individuals, points to Flávio Bolsonaro as involved, and accuses the commission's presidency of acting politically by closing proceedings without voting on the majority opinion.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro elogia 2º mandato de Trump e se vê como presidente do Brasil
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro praised Donald Trump's second term in the United States, calling it 'much better' than the first, and pledged that if elected president of Brazil in 2026, he would deliver an improved version of his father Jair Bolsonaro's administration.

item.title: Filiação de Jones Manoel ao PSOL é aprovada
item.description: The PSOL approved the affiliation of historian and YouTuber Jones Manoel, who will run for a federal deputy seat in Pernambuco in the 2026 elections. The party's national executive confirmed the decision, highlighting support from figures like Deputy Sâmia Bomfim and alignment with Lula's candidacy in the first round. The party hopes Jones Manoel will become the first federal deputy elected by PSOL in the Northeast.

item.title: Assista ao discurso de Flávio Bolsonaro nos EUA e leia a íntegra
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro spoke at CPAC in the U.S., arguing Brazil could become an ally by supplying rare minerals to reduce U.S. dependence on China. He compared his father Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation to Donald Trump’s, claiming political persecution via 'lawfare'. He criticized Lula for allegedly lobbying to prevent Brazil’s two largest drug cartels from being designated terrorist organizations, without naming PCC or Comando Vermelho, and accused the president and the PT of being 'openly anti-American'.

item.title: Flávio é ajudado por Eduardo em tradução de pergunta em inglês
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) required assistance from his brother Eduardo Bolsonaro to understand an English-language question posed by journalist Jayne Zirkle during CPAC in the U.S. The question asked what lessons America could learn from Brazil's elections. Flávio responded by criticizing Brazil's electoral system, claiming it is impossible to investigate alleged fraud due to restrictions on auditing the full process, and argued that both countries need to improve their electoral systems.

item.title: Em evento nos EUA, Flávio Bolsonaro pede pressão diplomática para que eleições tenham 'valores de origem americana'
item.description: At a conservative event in Texas, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro urged the U.S. to apply diplomatic pressure to ensure free and fair elections in Brazil in 2026, based on 'American-origin values'. He accused the Biden administration of interfering in the 2022 elections, compared his father Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation to 'lawfare', and called the former president Trump’s most loyal international ally. Flávio emphasized Brazil’s strategic importance in the Western Hemisphere due to its territory, population, economy, and critical mineral reserves.

item.title: Trumpismo de Flávio Bolsonaro fragiliza lero-lero da moderação
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro presents himself as a moderate figure of Bolsonarism, emphasizing marketing over a substantive government plan. He states that while he consults his father, Jair Bolsonaro, for decisions, he is the actual candidate, attempting to distance himself from the polarizing figure of the former president.

item.title: Único resultado da CPI foi jogar Lulinha no colo de Lula em pleno ano eleitoral
item.description: The INSS CPMI ended without substantive results, with its only practical effect being the political exposure of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), which inadvertently boosted Lula’s campaign by shifting focus away from Jair Bolsonaro’s culpability. The commission was marked by partisan maneuvers, such as the relator Alfredo Gaspar’s last-minute affiliation with the PL party, and serious allegations against him, including alleged rape of a minor, that were not pursued. The Planalto’s alternative report was rejected, and the CPMI failed to address the victims of the INSS fraud scheme, underscoring Congress’s prioritization of political games over accountability.

item.title: Roberto Carneiro assume Secretaria de Governo em São Paulo
item.description: Roberto Carneiro, São Paulo's Chief of Staff, will assume the State Government Secretariat, replacing Gilberto Kassab, who stepped down to organize the PSD for the 2026 elections. The appointment, to be published in the Official Gazette on March 30, is part of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' strategy to centralize political articulation. Nerylson Lima will replace Carneiro as Chief of Staff.

item.title: RJ: após liminar de Zanin, centro, direita e esquerda apoiam eleição direta
item.description: Following a temporary injunction by Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin that halted the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro's governor, leaders from center, right, and left-wing factions took to social media to advocate for a direct vote. This rare cross-ideological consensus underscores widespread concern over institutional instability in the state.

item.title: Moro critica “vergonhosa atuação” do governo Lula na CPMI do INSS
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro criticized the 'shameful performance' of the Lula government in the INSS CPMI, accusing the government bloc of trying to block investigations by rejecting the final report. He claimed the government sought to approve a separate vote that would protect President Lula’s son, Lulinha. Moro views this as an attempt to obstruct the inquiry into alleged irregularities.

item.title: Na CPAC, Flávio compara Bolsonaro a Trump e associa Lula a facções
item.description: At CPAC 2026, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro compared his father Jair Bolsonaro’s political trajectory to that of Donald Trump, accusing the Lula government of favoring criminal factions like the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) and Comando Vermelho, and pursuing a foreign policy aligned with China, Cuba, and Iran against U.S. interests. Without providing evidence, he claimed Lula lobbies for criminal groups and advocated for a realignment of Brazil with Washington. The speech also denounced Latin American leftism as an organized ideological bloc and predicted a right-wing victory in Brazil’s 2026 election.
